newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. Only routine RNS items: ongoing GBP500m buyback tranches (~107-110k shares repurchased and cancelled per day around June 2-5 via J.P. Morgan). Q1 2026 trading update (5% organic growth, guidance reconfirmed) was already known and reflected in yesterday's verdict. Today's -1.33% move is flow-driven drift, not fundamental.

sources.label: TipRanks - IMI buyback and cancellation RNS
DirectorsTalk - IMI reconfirms 2026 guidance after Q1 organic growth

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved IMI down to reconcile the adjusted-score rank (~42-43); nothing since June 6 changes the strict-screen evidence level, and the -1.33% day drift on routine buyback RNS is not a setup change. Hold at current rank.
verdicts.bull: Precision valve and hydronic-balancing franchise gives IMI a credible latent path into data-centre liquid cooling and thermal management; the GBP500m buyback plus reconfirmed mid-single-digit organic growth supports the floor while the AI-cooling optionality remains unpriced.
verdicts.neutral: IMI remains a diversified industrial where AI-adjacent cooling is a small, unquantified slice of revenue; +12.4% YTD is in line with quality industrials, so neither crowding nor dislocation creates a fresh alpha edge at rank 43.
verdicts.bear: The cooling pathway stays speculative with no disclosed AI/data-centre design wins; hyperscalers are standardizing on specialist CDU/coolant-loop suppliers, and IMI could be left with low-margin commodity valve content while Middle East disruption noted at Q1 nags at Process Automation.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Half-year results on 31 July 2026 - any quantification of data-centre/cooling order intake or a named hyperscaler-adjacent win would upgrade the latent-node evidence from medium to strong.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Thesis stays latent indefinitely: no AI-chain revenue disclosure materializes and the stock trades as a generic UK industrial, dead-weighting a strict AI-nodes screen.
